# CWE-1052 vulnerable: hard-coded database DSN literal
lang: go
file: CWE-1052-vulnerable.go
---
package sample

// CWE-1052: Excessive Use of Hard-Coded Literals in Initialization.
// Vulnerable: GORM opened with embedded username, password, and host strings.

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

func OpenAppDB() (*gorm.DB, error) {
	dsn := "host=db.internal user=app_admin password=SuperSecret99 dbname=orders sslmode=disable"
	return gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{})
}

func HealthWithDB() gin.HandlerFunc {
	db, _ := OpenAppDB()
	return func(c *gin.Context) {
		sqlDB, err := db.DB()
		if err != nil || sqlDB.Ping() != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusServiceUnavailable, gin.H{"db": "down"})
			return
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"db": "up"})
	}
}
